verification.label: LabKit state
verification.detail: Use LabKit JSON output to confirm the intended config or registry state.

limitations.label: Local evidence boundary
limitations.detail: LabKit can verify local configuration and the installed Codex registry. It cannot guarantee account, rollout, hosted service, or model-runtime behavior without fresh runtime evidence.

dependencies.label: Installed Codex CLI registry
dependencies.detail: The selected Codex CLI binary must expose this canonical feature key through `codex features list`.

limitations.label: Maturity label applies
limitations.detail: Under-development features are not ready for use; experimental features may change or disappear; deprecated and removed keys should not be newly enabled.

limitations.label: Feature flag only
limitations.detail: A true flag can unlock a local code path, but it does not install missing plugins, connect accounts, override managed requirements, or bypass server-side entitlement checks.

verification.label: Registry visibility
verification.detail: Confirm the installed Codex build still lists the canonical key and the expected maturity stage.

verification.label: Fresh session
verification.detail: Start a new Codex session after changing feature flags; existing sessions may have already resolved feature enablement.

dependencies.label: Codex config layer
dependencies.detail: Codex must load the `~/.codex/config.toml` or trusted project config layer where this setting is written.

verification.label: Config value
verification.detail: Confirm the selected top-level Codex setting has the intended value.

dependencies.label: Codex debug model catalog
dependencies.detail: LabKit must be able to run `codex debug models` to build a local model catalog override.

dependencies.label: Fresh Codex session
dependencies.detail: Codex reads model catalog and context settings when a session starts.

limitations.label: Metadata patch
limitations.detail: This changes local Codex model metadata and config. It does not grant model access, change your plan, or prove the hosted model accepts the full requested window.

verification.label: Layered runtime proof
verification.detail: Strict verification checks config, local catalog metadata, binary model view, and recent session logs with runtime context-window evidence.

1m-context.title: 1M Context
1m-context.description: Configures GPT-5.5 for the large local context profile.

dependencies.label: GPT-5.5 catalog entry
dependencies.detail: The selected Codex build must expose a GPT-5.5 model entry that LabKit can patch locally.

dependencies.label: Model eligibility
dependencies.detail: Your account and current provider route must actually be allowed to use the selected model and large context.

limitations.label: Effective window reserve
limitations.detail: Codex can reserve part of the configured context window for runtime safety and UI estimates, so the visible usable context can be lower than 1,000,000 tokens.

limitations.label: Disable is manual
limitations.detail: LabKit does not automatically undo the catalog patch. Restore a backup or remove `model_catalog_json` and related context settings manually.

verification.label: Session evidence
verification.detail: Run a fresh Codex session and then scan recent session logs for `model_context_window` evidence near the expected effective window.

web-search-live.title: Live Web Search
web-search-live.description: Requests live search instead of cached search behavior.

dependencies.label: Network access
dependencies.detail: Live search requires the active Codex session policy and sandbox to allow network use.

limitations.label: Untrusted web content
limitations.detail: OpenAI documents cached mode as reducing exposure to prompt injection from arbitrary live content; live results should still be treated as untrusted.

verification.label: Transcript evidence
verification.detail: Look for `web_search` items in the transcript or `codex exec --json` output when Codex searches.

memories.title: Memories
memories.description: Enables the local memory switch when the current build exposes it.

goals.title: Goals
goals.description: Tracks a concrete thread objective until the work is complete.

codex_hooks.name: codex-hooks
codex_hooks.title: Hooks
codex_hooks.description: Runs local lifecycle hooks when the current build gates them.

external_migration.name: external-migration
external_migration.title: External Migration
external_migration.description: Imports external agent session state where supported.

prevent_idle_sleep.name: prevent-idle-sleep
prevent_idle_sleep.title: Prevent Idle Sleep
prevent_idle_sleep.description: Prevents idle sleep during active work on supported macOS setups.

terminal_resize_reflow.name: terminal-resize-reflow
terminal_resize_reflow.title: Terminal Resize Reflow
terminal_resize_reflow.description: Reflows terminal output more cleanly after width changes.

apps.title: Connectors
apps.description: Shows connector-backed tools and app integrations when available.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: Lets installed plugin bundles contribute skills, tools, and integrations.

tool_suggest.name: tool-suggest
tool_suggest.title: Tool Suggest
tool_suggest.description: Surfaces relevant tools or connectors when a task appears to need them.

browser_use.name: browser-use
browser_use.title: Browser Use
browser_use.description: Lets agents inspect pages, click flows, and capture UI evidence.

dependencies.label: Requirements may gate availability
dependencies.detail: Codex requirements can pin `features.browser_use` false, which disables Browser Use and Browser Agent availability.

dependencies.label: Browser integration
dependencies.detail: The relevant browser/plugin surface must be installed and usable for a real browser task to succeed.

limitations.label: Availability vs. action
limitations.detail: Enabling this flag can make the integration available; it does not prove every site, profile, auth, or browser action will work.

verification.label: Browser smoke test
verification.detail: Run a fresh Codex session and ask it to inspect a simple local or public page, then confirm a browser-use transcript/tool event appears.

computer_use.name: computer-use
computer_use.title: Computer Use
computer_use.description: Lets agents drive supported desktop surfaces under the normal permission model.

dependencies.label: Requirements may gate availability
dependencies.detail: Codex requirements can pin `features.computer_use` false, which disables Computer Use availability and related install or enablement flows.

dependencies.label: Supported desktop surface
dependencies.detail: The current Codex surface, OS permissions, and desktop automation integration must support computer-use actions.

limitations.label: No permission bypass
limitations.detail: The flag does not bypass Codex approval policy, OS accessibility permissions, or product availability gates.

verification.label: Desktop smoke test
verification.detail: Run a fresh Codex session and perform a harmless supported desktop action, then check transcript/tool evidence.

multi_agent.name: multi-agent
multi_agent.title: Multi-Agent
multi_agent.description: Lets a main agent delegate bounded work to helper agents.

verification.label: Delegation evidence
verification.detail: A real proof requires a session that successfully starts and receives output from a helper agent.

personality.title: Personality
personality.description: Enables interaction-style controls exposed by the current build.

enable_request_compression.name: request-compression
enable_request_compression.title: Request Compression
enable_request_compression.description: Reduces payload size for larger and tool-heavy turns.

shell_snapshot.name: shell-snapshot
shell_snapshot.title: Shell Snapshot
shell_snapshot.description: Preserves more accurate terminal context across tool calls.

shell_tool.name: shell-tool
shell_tool.title: Shell Tool
shell_tool.description: Keeps shell execution available when gated by a feature flag.

unified_exec.name: unified-exec
unified_exec.title: Unified Exec
unified_exec.description: Uses the newer command-execution path for terminal sessions.

remote_control.name: remote-control
remote_control.title: Remote Control
remote_control.description: Exposes remote-control support when the installed Codex CLI build lists it.

enable_fanout.name: enable-fanout
enable_fanout.title: Fanout Agent Jobs
enable_fanout.description: Enables CSV-backed agent job tools when the installed Codex build lists them.

dependencies.label: multi_agent
dependencies.detail: The upstream Codex feature resolver enables `multi_agent` when `enable_fanout` is enabled.

code_mode_only.name: code-mode-only
code_mode_only.title: Code Mode Only
code_mode_only.description: Restricts model-visible tools to code-mode entrypoints when the installed Codex build lists it.

dependencies.label: code_mode
dependencies.detail: The upstream Codex feature resolver enables `code_mode` when `code_mode_only` is enabled.

limitations.label: Under development
limitations.detail: Upstream marks this feature under development in current source; do not treat it as production-stable.
